title: Lost Pig
author: Admiral Jota
desc: You are Grunk. Grunk lose pig. Boss say find pig. That all Grunk know.

title: Photopia
author: Adam Cadre
desc: Interactive poetry about light, loss, and the colors between. Not really a game. Something better.

title: Spider and Web
author: Andrew Plotkin
desc: An interrogation. A flashback. Nothing is what it seems. The original unreliable narrator game.

title: Anchorhead
author: Michael Gentry
desc: Lovecraftian horror in a small New England town. Your husband inherited a mansion. You inherited the nightmares.

title: Shade
author: Andrew Plotkin
desc: One room. One apartment. Something is very wrong and it's getting worse. Paranoia in text form.

title: Dungeon
author: Tim Anderson, Marc Blank, Bruce Daniels, Dave Lebling
desc: The original mainframe Zork. The Great Underground Empire before it was split into three retail games.

title: Zork I: The Great Underground Empire
author: Infocom
desc: West of house. You are standing in an open field. There is a mailbox here. You know the rest.

title: Zork II: The Wizard of Frobozz
author: Infocom
desc: Deeper into the Great Underground Empire. The Wizard is watching. The puzzles are harder.

title: Zork III: The Dungeon Master
author: Infocom
desc: The final chapter. Less treasure hunting, more existential. What does it mean to be the Dungeon Master?

title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
author: Douglas Adams & Steve Meretzky
desc: The Earth is about to be demolished. You have a hangover. The puzzle with the babel fish will make you cry.

title: Planetfall
author: Steve Meretzky
desc: Stranded on a deserted planet with Floyd, the most lovable robot in fiction. You will feel things.

title: Trinity
author: Brian Moriarty
desc: Nuclear weapons, time travel, and a white door that appears in a London park. Infocom's masterpiece.

title: A Mind Forever Voyaging
author: Steve Meretzky
desc: You're an AI simulating the future. The future is getting worse. Political sci-fi decades ahead of its time.

title: Wishbringer
author: Brian Moriarty
desc: A gentle fairy tale that eases you into interactive fiction. Deliver a letter. Save a town. Use a magic stone.

title: Endless, Nameless
author: Adam Cadre
desc: From the creator of Photopia. A game that keeps changing what kind of game it is.

title: Counterfeit Monkey
author: Emily Short
desc: Remove a letter from 'sword' and get a 'word'. The most inventive wordplay mechanic in any game, ever.

title: Galatea
author: Emily Short
desc: A conversation with a living statue. Seventy possible endings. The best dialogue system in IF history.

title: Violet
author: Jeremy Freese
desc: Write 1000 words or your girlfriend leaves you. Your office is full of distractions. Sound familiar?

title: Blue Lacuna
author: Aaron A. Reed
desc: A novel-length interactive story on a mysterious island. Art vs love. One of the longest IF works ever made.

title: Slouching Towards Bedlam
author: Star Foster & Daniel Ravipinto
desc: Victorian steampunk Bethlem asylum. An alien intelligence is spreading through language itself.

title: Coloratura
author: Lynnea Glasser
desc: You're the alien on the ship. The humans are the monsters. Everything is color and song and misunderstanding.

title: The Wizard Sniffer
author: Buster Hudson
desc: Fantasy comedy. You're a pig. You can smell wizards. It's exactly as good as that sounds.

title: Dr Ludwig and the Devil
author: SV Linwood
desc: A Faustian bargain with excellent comedy timing. Sell your soul, negotiate the terms, find the loopholes.

title: Dr Horror's House of Terror
author: Ade McT
desc: B-movie horror comedy. Cheesy, self-aware, and surprisingly clever. Bring popcorn.

title: Superluminal
author: C.E.J. Pacian
desc: Faster-than-light space exploration with a streamlined interface. Short, sweet, and beautifully designed.

title: The Bat
author: Jess Mersky
desc: A bat gets into your house. Things escalate.

title: And Then You Come to a House Not Unlike the Previous One
author: B.J. Best
desc: Procedurally generated rooms, hand-crafted melancholy. A house that keeps going.

title: Repeat the Ending
author: Drew Cook
desc: Time loop puzzle. Figure out what happened. Figure out how to fix it. Repeat.

title: What Heart Heard of, Ghost Guessed
author: Amanda Walker
desc: A ghost story told through poetry and fragments. Atmospheric and haunting.

title: According to Cain
author: Jim Nelson
desc: Noir detective fiction. Dark, well-written, and dripping with atmosphere. TADS format - may not play in browser.
